verdict_reasoning: KLK2 is predominantly secreted into prostatic fluid under baseline conditions. However, recent 2025 evidence establishes that KLK2 is expressed on the cell surface of prostate cancer cells, confirmed by IHC, immunofluorescence with EpCAM co-localization, and direct antibody binding studies. Multiple cell-surface-directed therapeutics (bispecific T-cell engager pasritamig, CAR-T, radioligand therapy, ADCs) have entered clinical trials, proving accessibility from the extracellular face. Surface expression appears restricted to the prostate cancer context rather than baseline normal prostate—a cell-state-induced phenomenon.
